markets.question: Spread: Moreirense FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primeira Liga game, scheduled for May 2 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Moreirense FC" if Moreirense FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "CF Estrela da Amadora".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaportugal.pt. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Moreirense FC vs. CF Estrela da Amadora - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primeira Liga game, scheduled for May 2 at 10:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Moreirense hold a solid seventh place in the Primeira Liga standings with 39 points from 30 matches, bolstered by a recent 1-0 home win over Estoril that ended a three-game skid, showcasing defensive resilience at Parque de Jogos Comendador Joaquim de Almeida Freitas. Estrela da Amadora languish in 15th, fighting relegation with a poor away record, following their goalless draw against Moreirense in December. Head-to-head favors Moreirense overall (5 wins in 10), though recent meetings are tight. Minor injuries sideline Moreirense's Vasco Sousa (long-term fibula) and Estrela's Jefferson Encada (muscle since March), but full squads expected; late-season stakes heighten Estrela's motivation amid table pressures and home/away splits.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and JD Gaming each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Team WE vs JD Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Team WE and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 2 at 3:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against JD Gaming.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win the match against Team WE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: JD Gaming holds a commanding edge in trader consensus for this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch against Team WE, fueled by their 2-0 sweep over WE just one week ago on April 25, where jungler JunJia earned Player of the Match honors with dominant Jarvan IV plays. JDG rides a hot 5-1 streak across their last six series, contrasting WE's winless 0-6 skid and #36 world ranking, hampered by inconsistent macro and objective execution. Lifetime head-to-head tilts heavily JDG's way (20-7), amplified by superior roster depth featuring Xiaoxu top, GALA ADC, and stable midlane control from HongQ. Both squads chase seeding in the eight-team round-robin, with no reported injuries, subs, or patch-altering metas shifting dynamics pre-match.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds a commanding position in LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend standings at 6-3, bolstered by a recent 2-0 sweep over Top Esports that extended their win streak to four series, positioning them firmly in top-four playoff bracket contention amid the double round-robin BO3 format with Fearless Draft. Invictus Gaming languishes at 4-5, scrambling for play-in survival after a 2-1 win over Weibo Gaming but hampered by an earlier 0-2 loss to AL this split and overall inconsistent synergy from new additions like top laner Breathe and ADC JiaQi alongside veteran mid Rookie. AL's stable roster—Flandre, Tarzan, Shanks, Hope, Kael—excels in patch 26.7 meta favoring early aggression, giving them head-to-head edge in this rematch.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team Vitality and SK Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Vitality enters their Week 6 LEC Spring regular season BO3 against SK Gaming with a dominant 6-1 record, already the second team to clinch playoffs behind undefeated Karmine Corp, reflecting their six-series win streak including recent 2-0 sweeps over GIANTX and Team Heretics. SK Gaming languishes at 2-6, mired in four consecutive series losses to top contenders like G2 Esports and Karmine Corp, underscoring struggles with their revamped roster of veterans Wunder, Skeanz, Lider, Jopa, and Mikyx. No roster changes or player absences reported amid the current patch's macro-focused meta; Vitality's superior form and earlier Versus win over SK shape trader consensus, though SK could exploit any draft missteps in this survival matchup.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team Vitality and SK Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Leviatan Esports and paiN Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Honor of Kings Challenger Cup Stage 1 single-elimination bracket, KPL powerhouse AG Super Play faces rising challenger squad WST in a pivotal BO7 matchup on May 2, determining advancement to the quarterfinals. AG Super Play, boasting veterans like YiNuo and recent roster additions XiaoYu and Jiushu, scraped through Round 1 with a gritty 3-2 comeback over RW Mania four days ago, showcasing resilience amid a subpar KPL Spring where they failed to defend their title. WST, featuring loaned jungler BaiLian and midlaner XiaoHan, pulled off an upset BO5 win against CKU, leveraging aggressive macro play but lacking AG's pedigree and depth. No roster changes or injuries reported; trader consensus reflects AG's superior head-to-head experience and map control edge in extended BO7 series, though WST's momentum poses upset potential in this high-stakes knockout.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Basque derby at Mendizorrotza on May 2, Alavés (16th, 36 points) host mid-table Athletic Bilbao (10th, 41 points) amid mixed recent form—Alavés with wins over Celta and Mallorca, Bilbao beating Osasuna but losing to Atlético. markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Cape Town be 14°C on May 4?
markets.description:

markets.question: Will Anthropic’s market cap be between 0.9T and 1.2T at market close on IPO day by December 31 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Anthropic's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2027".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Anthropic’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

markets.question: Will "Bully - Ye" be the Billboard 200 #1 album for the week of May 9?
markets.description:
Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then dated “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”.

This market will resolve according to the number 1 album on the Billboard 200 chart dated “Week of May 9, 2026”.

Levante's defensive frailties deepen with suspensions for Iván Romero and injuries to Kareem Tunde, Roger Brugué, Elgezabal, and Unai Vencedor, likely prompting a low-block approach that inflates corners against Villarreal's possession-dominant home style averaging 9.6 total corners per game. Levante away fixtures average 10.06 corners, aligning with La Liga's 9.7 league-wide mark and their own 3.9 corners won per match, setting up potential for elevated set-piece volume amid Villarreal's push for European spots.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy entered the United21 Season 47 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinal BO3 against Infinite as Group A winners with a perfect 2-0 record, posting a dominant +16 map differential via close 2-1 win over LPH Gaming and 2-0 rout of Sangal ALTERS. Infinite scraped through Group D as runners-up (2-1 overall, -9 differential), rebounding with a decider 2-1 over AaB esport after a 0-2 group loss to Misa, relying on stand-ins like fnl and SHiPZ. No head-to-head history; both academy squads boast stable rosters—Imperial's ta9z, sh1Fu, Kragh trio driving momentum—shaping trader consensus around Imperial's superior form in the European online circuit ahead of the April 9 clash.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market favors California Gov. Gavin Newsom at 26.3% implied probability, driven by his national fundraising surge since late 2025, executive experience, and term-limited exit after the November 2026 midterms, positioning him as a steady frontrunner amid post-2024 party recalibration. Former Vice President Kamala Harris climbed to 10.8% after signaling a potential comeback bid on April 10 at the National Action Network and leading a Harvard CAPS/Harris poll (April 23-26) 50%-22% among Democrats, though markets diverge from this outlier showing trader skepticism over her 2024 loss. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's 8.5% reflects progressive youth appeal, while Jon Ossoff (6.6%) and Pete Buttigieg (4.1%) benefit from Senate and prior campaign profiles. Consolidation awaits 2026 midterm results, early primary polling in Iowa and New Hampshire, and factional endorsements.

markets.question: Will Bernie Sanders say "Corrupt" or "Corruption" in Michigan?
markets.description:
Bernie Sanders is scheduled to speak in Detroit, Michigan on his Fighting Oligarchy tour on May 3, 2026 (https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/rsvp-oligarchy-detroit/?source=website-oligarchy). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bernie Sanders says the listed term during events in Detroit scheduled for May 3, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old interviews or prerecorded videos are aired where Sanders is speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market is explicitly about Bernie Sanders's remarks in Detroit scheduled for May 3, 2026. Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of the named, scheduled event will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If this event is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, "-No Qualifying Event-" will resolve to "Yes" and all other brackets will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Taylor Pendrith win the 2026 Cadillac Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Cadillac Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Cadillac Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Cadillac Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 9, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

Jordan Spieth (6.5%) and Nick Taylor (2.5%) lurk at 8-under, with Alex Smalley (3.1%) also contending, though Young's scrambling (80%) and prior 36-hole leads (1-1 record) solidify his edge heading into the weekend.

markets.question: Will "Octane - Don Toliver" be the Billboard 200 #1 album for the week of May 9?
markets.description:
Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then dated “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”.

events.title: Which DCMs self-certify sports event contracts by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: CFTC Division of Market Oversight's March 12 advisory has spurred designated contract markets (DCMs) to pursue self-certification of sports event contracts, stressing pre-filing engagement with leagues like MLB—which recently inked an MOU—robust manipulation surveillance, official data settlement, and comprehensive compliance demos to preempt stays. After withdrawing restrictive event contract rules in February, the agency signaled support, with CME already filing successfully. ANPRM comments closed April 30 amid Kalshi legal wins affirming DCM preemption over state gambling laws. Traders monitor filings from LedgerX and others by June 30 deadline, as league tie-ups and CFTC reviews could tip balances in this nascent prediction market arena.

markets.question: Will Meta have the #1 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 83% implied probability to Anthropic claiming the #1 AI model on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard (Style Control On) by May 31, driven by Claude Opus 4.7's mid-April release that propelled it to the top with an Elo score around 1505, excelling in coding, reasoning, and sustained tasks where style-neutral evaluation favors its substantive outputs over stylistic flair. eventMetadata.context_description: With Wolverhampton Wanderers and Burnley mathematically relegated after dismal campaigns—Wolves with just 18 points from 35 games and Burnley on 20—the Premier League relegation battle hinges on the final third spot among Tottenham Hotspur (34 points from 34 matches, GD -10), West Ham United (36 points from 35, GD -19), and Nottingham Forest (39 points from 34, GD -4). West Ham's damaging 3-0 defeat at Brentford yesterday keeps them precariously placed, opening the door for Spurs to climb out with a win at Aston Villa tomorrow amid their extended winless run. Forest boast three wins in their last four but face tough remaining fixtures against Chelsea (A), Newcastle (H), Manchester United (A), and Bournemouth (H), compounded by Europa League semis fatigue, while head-to-head results and goal difference could prove decisive in the run-in.

markets.question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be $1.5T or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 73.5% implied probability against an OpenAI IPO by December 31, 2026, propelled by Wall Street Journal reports from late April revealing missed revenue and user targets despite CEO Sam Altman's push for a Q4 listing. CFO Sarah Friar has voiced internal concerns over the aggressive timeline, highlighting unsustainable $600 billion compute commitments over five years, slowing growth post-$122 billion funding at an $852 billion private valuation, and organizational gaps—prompting her sidelining from key investor discussions. Elon Musk's ongoing lawsuit further clouds restructuring, while high-cap outcomes like 1.5T+ (10.9%) reflect optimism tempered by AI competitive pressures from Anthropic and xAI; watch trial outcomes and potential S-1 filings for shifts.

markets.question: Ethereum Up or Down on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT May 1 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the May 2 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT May 1 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the May 2 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (LOW) $3.75 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Adam Miller win the 2026 Los Angeles mayoral election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Los Angeles mayoral election will be held on June 2, 2026, to elect the mayor of Los Angeles, California. If no candidate receives a majority of the vote, a runoff election will be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins the election.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the City of Los Angeles.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the closely contested Los Angeles mayoral nonpartisan primary on June 2, 2026, trader consensus on Polymarket positions City Councilmember Nithya Raman as the frontrunner at 49.5% implied probability, driven by her recent fundraising surge alongside reality TV personality Spencer Pratt and a controversial March poll showing her leading incumbent Mayor Karen Bass. High voter uncertainty persists, with a UCLA Luskin poll last week revealing 40% undecided amid Bass's unfavorable ratings tied to homelessness and recent wildfires. Pratt's viral campaign ads and outsider appeal as a Palisades Fire survivor have propelled him to 21.5%, edging Bass at 20.5%, as recent celebrity endorsements and Raman's May 1 statement vowing to exit politics if unsuccessful underscore the race's volatility ahead of early voting.

markets.question: Will the next model released by OpenAI debut at a score of at least 1500?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next model released by OpenAI has at least the specified score on the Arena.AI Leaderboard (arena.ai/leaderboard/text) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on the date after the day of the release, 12:00 PM ET.  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

If no qualifying score for the specified model is available on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If no qualifying score becomes available by the end of the seventh day following the day of the model’s release, or if no qualifying model release occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

If multiple models are released on the same calendar date or if multiple variants of the specified model appear on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at the relevant check time (e.g., base, “Thinking,” or “Instant”), the highest-scoring variant will be used for resolution.

A qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at arena.ai/leaderboard/text. If this resolution source is unavailable at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If it remains unavailable through the end of the seventh day after a qualifying release, it will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 80.5% implied probability against a Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, driven by stalled progress following the company's confidential S-1 filing in January 2026 with Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan as underwriters. Initial March debut hopes faded amid persistent monetization challenges—revenue trails at around $800 million annually despite 200 million monthly active users—coupled with a youth-skewed base limiting enterprise adoption versus peers like Slack. A compressed two-month window to roadshow, pricing, and listing amplifies execution risks, relegating sub-$15 billion outcomes to 16.3% while higher brackets reflect scant optimism for premium valuations in a sobering SaaS environment. Watch for public S-1 emergence as the pivotal near-term catalyst.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping purge Wang Huning in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if either of the following conditions are met by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

1) The listed individual is removed or resigns from their primary political post or from their position, if any, on the Chinese Politburo or its standing committee, with a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor.

2) The listed individual is expelled from the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). 

Qualifying announcements and reporting of a purge before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal/expulsion goes into effect.

A listed individual leaving office at the end of a regularly scheduled term, or being removed/resigning from office without a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor, will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from the Chinese Government and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Xi Jinping's anti-corruption campaign has driven sweeping purges of senior People's Liberation Army (PLA) officers throughout 2026, with the Central Military Commission (CMC) reduced to two members after investigations into top generals like Zhang Youxia in January and subsequent removals of nine military lawmakers in February ahead of the Two Sessions. In March, Xi publicly stressed political loyalty amid empty delegate seats, while April reports confirmed probes into a Politburo member—the widest such action since 1976—signaling expansion beyond the military. These developments, rooted in ensuring absolute obedience via the Central Commission for Discipline Inspection, heighten uncertainty for remaining high-ranking officials ahead of the 2027 Party Congress, as traders weigh loyalty risks and factional pressures in this opaque process.

markets.question: Will Jared Kushner attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran direct diplomatic talks, the highest-level since the 1979 revolution, stalled after the latest Islamabad round in late April 2026 without agreement on Iran's nuclear program or Strait of Hormuz access, amid a fragile ceasefire from the ongoing 2026 Iran war. President Donald Trump stated on May 2 he is dissatisfied with Tehran's newest proposal submitted via Pakistan mediators, signaling no immediate next meeting while phone diplomacy persists and escalation risks rise ahead of potential ceasefire expiration. Prior sessions featured US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi as lead negotiators, with traders watching for similar delegations or surprises like Jared Kushner involvement, as third-country hosts like Oman or Pakistan facilitate amid heightened rhetoric.

markets.question: Will Orlando City SC win on 2026-05-02?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 2, 2026
If Orlando City SC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Inter Miami CF vs. Orlando City SC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming MLS game, scheduled for Saturday, May 2, 2026 between Inter Miami CF and Orlando City SC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Inter Miami CF enters this Florida Derby as heavy trader favorites at 75.5% implied probability, driven by their second-place standing in the Eastern Conference with 19 points from 10 matches, including a robust recent form of LWWW DW and home advantage at Nu Stadium. Lionel Messi's availability bolsters their attack, despite hamstring absences for Sergio Reguilón and Mateo Silvetti. Orlando City SC, mired in 14th place with a 2-1-7 record and recent LLLWL slump, faces mounting injury concerns—OUTs for Joran Gerbet and Marco Pašalić, plus questionables like Eduard Atuesta, David Brekalo, and Duncan McGuire—hampering their upset bid at 10.5%, while draw pricing at 13.5% reflects the rivalry's historical evenness (7-7-5 H2H) but current mismatch.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "FC Barcelona" if FC Barcelona win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Bayern München".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona Femení hold a commanding position in Liga F with a near-perfect 24-0-1 record, propelled by recent thumpings of Real Madrid including a 3-0 quarter-final win on March 29 to secure their UEFA Women's Champions League semi-final spot against Bayern Frauen. Bayern, unbeaten domestically this season atop the Frauen-Bundesliga save for a 7-1 group-stage drubbing by Barcelona in October 2025, advanced via a gritty 5-3 aggregate over Manchester City with late strikes from Viggósdóttir and Dallmann. First leg on April 25 in Munich favors Bayern's home crowd and revenge motive, but Barcelona's depth shines despite Aitana Bonmatí's ongoing fibula absence until early May; Bayern miss winger Klara Bühl (muscle injury). Head-to-head favors Barça (4-1-0), underscoring trader focus on aggregate dynamics, clean sheets, and Salma Paralluelo's form.

markets.question: Will Louisiana use a new congressional map for the 2026 United States midterm elections?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed US state uses a new congressional district map for the 2026 United States midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

To qualify, after the 2024 United States congressional elections, the listed state must have adopted a new congressional district map that is:
- Formally adopted and enacted into law by the appropriate legislative or redistricting authority;
- Not enjoined, vacated or otherwise fully struck down prior to the 2026 United States Midterm elections; and
- In effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are temporarily stayed pending appeal but later upheld will qualify if they are in effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are completely redrawn by a court, special master, or legislature before implementation will not qualify, unless the redrawn map is itself a new map used in the 2026 United States midterm elections. 

If the 2026 United States midterm elections occur and no new qualifying map is in use, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the listed state and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Several states have enacted new congressional maps for the November 2026 midterms through mid-decade redistricting, including California, Missouri, North Carolina, Ohio, Texas, Utah, and Virginia, primarily via legislative action or court orders since summer 2025. Virginia voters approved a Democratic-favoring constitutional amendment on April 21, 2026, enabling its map despite recent court challenges resolved in late April. Florida's legislature passed a Republican-leaning map on April 29 amid a special session triggered by the U.S. Supreme Court's same-day ruling in Louisiana v. Callais, which struck down that state's map as a racial gerrymander and weakened Voting Rights Act constraints, potentially spurring changes in Louisiana, Mississippi, and Tennessee. Ongoing litigation, including Missouri's state supreme court hearing on May 14, and approaching primaries heighten uncertainty over final map usage.

markets.question: Will Jonas Wind be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Goals scored in Bundesliga matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., DFB-Pokal, German Super Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 Bundesliga season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Bundesliga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane commands a 33-18 goal lead over Deniz Undav after 31 matchdays of the 2025/26 Bundesliga season, cementing his 99.4% implied probability as top goalscorer amid Bayern Munich's title-clinching run. Recent strikes against Mainz on April 25 and Stuttgart on April 19 have widened the gap, with challengers like Serhou Guirassy, Michael Olise, and Lois Openda stuck in single digits relative to Kane's haul. Bayern's secured Bundesliga crown allows potential rest, but the 15-goal buffer leaves no mathematical path for pursuers even with hat-tricks in the final three fixtures. Only a severe Kane injury sidelining him entirely, paired with freak scoring outbursts, could realistically challenge trader consensus.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy, Team Spirit's academy squad with a revamped roster since February featuring riflers VILBy, mazay, and Kiryasoo, entered Exort Series #25 Main Stage Round 1 as a higher-tier contender after mixed results in prior C-Tier events like Winline Insight. Wave Esports, HLTV-ranked #149 with recent additions like Belof and sheezy, advanced from Contenders Stage (3-1 record) but carried a 37.5% three-month win rate amid inconsistent form in qualifiers like Urban Riga Open. No roster changes or patch impacts reported pre-match; trader consensus weighed Spirit's map pool edges on Mirage and Inferno against Wave's underdog upset potential in BO3 swiss format, with no major news in the prior 48 hours.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the VA-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the VA-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Deportivo Alavés vs. Athletic Club - More Markets

eventMetadata.context_description: With La Liga round 34 looming on May 2 at Estadio de Mendizorroza, relegation-battling Deportivo Alavés (17th, 33 points from 31 matches, -11 goal difference) host mid-table Athletic Club (12th, struggling with 11 wins, 5 draws, 15 losses). Athletic arrive off a 1-2 loss to Villarreal, with poor away splits and no victory in last five derbies after Alavés' shock 1-0 September clean sheet. Watch injury updates—Alavés without Benat Prados (knee) and Ander Gorosabel (hip), Athletic nursing Unai Egiluz long-term—plus Basque rivalry intensity and table pressure shaping trader consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino's confirmation at the recent FIFA Congress that Iran will participate in the 2026 FIFA World Cup and play its Group G matches on US soil has solidified trader consensus at 87.5% for no replacement, prioritizing football over geopolitical tensions from the US-Iran conflict. This follows Iran's earlier signals of potential withdrawal and rejected requests to relocate games to Mexico, alongside a swiftly dismissed US envoy proposal to slot in Italy—which Azzurri officials called "shameful" and inappropriate given sporting merit. Lingering 7.2% on Italy reflects that brief speculation, while low odds on UAE or Bolivia stem from AFC qualification protocols favoring regional runners-up like Iraq. Realistic challenges include a last-minute Iranian boycott, US visa denials for IRGC-linked players, or FIFA rule changes amid escalating security risks before June kickoff.

markets.question: Will Encore Medical’s market cap be between $50M and $55M at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Encore Medical’s market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

As of market creation, the IPO is scheduled to price on April 22 (ET). If no such IPO occurs by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before June 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency.

It is calculated as the total number of outstanding shares, multiplied by the official closing share price of the publicly traded class on the first trading day.

If necessary, to accurately capture the company’s total market capitalization, rather than a stock-class-specific market capitalization, the calculation will include all outstanding share classes and apply any stated conversion ratios to the publicly traded class. Where no conversion right exists, such shares will be counted at their stated outstanding amount without discount, unless official filings explicitly specify differently.

The number of outstanding shares will be determined from official company filings or disclosures (e.g., SEC filings). The closing share price on the first trading day will be determined from the primary exchange’s official listing page.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official company filings and the primary exchange’s official listing page. The market capitalization will be determined through appropriate calculation using the total outstanding shares and the closing price from the first day of trading.

In the event of an interruption in the normal trading session on the specified company’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that day as the first day of trading for the purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 37.5% implied probability for no Encore Medical (EMI) IPO before June 2026, reflecting prolonged SEC review since the initial S-1 filing in September 2025 and absence of a firm pricing date despite terms set in December 2025 for a $15 million raise via 3 million shares at $5 each, implying a pro forma market cap around $49 million on 9.74 million fully diluted shares. Conditional on completion, probabilities cluster in the $45–55 million range (51.6% combined), discounting weak fundamentals—$2.1 million trailing twelve-month revenue up 48.8% year-over-year but net losses of $1.58 million, pre-U.S. commercialization ahead of FDA pivotal trial results targeted for end-2027—amid competitive pressures from Abbott and Boston Scientific in the $1.5 billion PFO closure market. Micro-cap medtech volatility and soft IPO sentiment for loss-making firms further cap upside, with 55 million-plus at just 1.8%; watch for pricing announcements or trial updates as key catalysts.

Amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict from February airstrikes and Strait of Hormuz tensions, no US ground forces entered Iran; WTI crude oil prices hovered around $100 per barrel without spiking to $200; the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady at its April 29 FOMC meeting; President Trump's rhetoric and Pentagon planning on Cuba yielded no military action after Senate procedural blocks; and no individuals were jailed over Epstein files disclosures, despite earlier releases and investigations. With verifiable public records confirming the absence of events, resolution to "Nothing" appears locked in, though a disputed oracle proposal could theoretically challenge interpretations of criteria like "military action" or "Fed decides any change."

markets.question: Will Meta have the second best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) hit (HIGH) $435 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Republicanos (REPUBLICANOS) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Partido Liberal (PL) to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 seats (two-thirds of the 81 total) are contested across 27 states, reflecting PL candidates' leads in recent state-level polls like Paraná Pesquisas in Rio de Janeiro (Cláudio Castro or Rogéria Bolsonaro topping at 29-32%) and VOX Brasil in São Paulo (Guilherme Derrite at 27%). This positioning builds on PL's momentum from the 2024 municipal elections, where it garnered the highest vote share for mayors despite centrão parties like PSD and MDB electing more prefeitos, and its January 2026 ascent to the Senate's largest bloc with 15 senators via defections. NOVO and União Brasil trail with competitive prospects in states like Minas Gerais and others, per aggregated projections, amid Flávio Bolsonaro's strong presidential polling boosting PL turnout. markets.question: Will China GDP growth in Q2 2026 be between 4.0% and 4.3%?

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Madrid be 20°C on May 4?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts cluster tightly around 20–22°C for Madrid's highest temperature on May 4 at Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport, driving the near-even trader consensus with 36% implied probability for 20°C, 35.5% each for 21°C and 22°C. This reflects typical early May climatology, where average highs rise from 20°C toward 23–25°C later in the month amid strengthening subtropical ridging, but current model runs depict subtle differences—stronger boundary-layer mixing and clearer skies could favor 21–22°C, while increased high-cloud cover or light westerly shear caps peaks nearer 20°C. AEMET guidance aligns with this range under partly cloudy conditions and light winds. Watch daily ECMWF updates and AEMET revisions for shifts in the 48-hour outlook, as spring diurnal heating adds uncertainty.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis, term-limited out of office in January 2027, faces slim odds of joining the Trump administration by June 30 amid unconfirmed rumors rather than official actions. Recent Axios reporting on April 21 cited sources claiming DeSantis is seeking high-profile roles like Attorney General, Secretary of Defense, or even a Supreme Court seat, prompting Trump to tell reporters on May 1 that he "likes him a lot" and would consider a cabinet spot. However, no nomination, resignation announcement, or formal discussions have materialized in the past week, with DeSantis remaining focused on state duties. Traders' strong 86% No consensus reflects the tight two-month window, historical patterns of early-term cabinet fillings, and absence of momentum for a mid-governorship exit.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (HIGH) $7.00 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "New York Mets" if the New York Mets win the game by 2 or more runs.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Los Angeles Angels". If the game ends in a tie, this market will resolve to "Los Angeles Angels".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: New York Mets vs. Los Angeles Angels
events.description:

This market will resolve to "New York Mets" if the New York Mets win the game.

This market will resolve to "Los Angeles Angels" if the Los Angeles Angels win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Mets head to Angel Stadium for the second game of their interleague series against the Los Angeles Angels after rallying for a 4-3 win in Thursday's opener behind Ronny Mauricio's go-ahead homer, snapping a six-game road skid. Probable starters Nolan McLean (1-2, 2.55 ERA, 45 strikeouts in 35 1/3 innings) and Reid Detmers (1-2, 4.28 ERA) set up a pitcher's duel between two young arms, with McLean's elite 0.85 WHIP giving the Mets an edge despite their league-worst 3.25 runs per game amid injuries to Francisco Lindor (calf), Jorge Polanco, and Luis Robert Jr. (back). The Angels, mired below .500 with their own absences like Logan O'Hoppe (wrist), struggle offensively at 3.7 runs per game, favoring low-scoring trends in this home matchup.
